task_completion.description: The email client application is open with the folder pane visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email client window is open on the screen, showing a sidebar or pane with mail accounts and folders.

task_completion.description: The 'Local Folders' account is located and interacted with.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Local Folders' item in the folder pane is visible and either clicked, highlighted, or has a context menu opened over it.

task_completion.description: The dialog or prompt to create a new folder is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Folder' dialog box, pop-up, or inline text input field is visible on the screen, specifically associated with the 'Local Folders' account.

task_completion.description: The name 'Work' is typed into the folder creation input.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Work' is visible inside the text input field for the new folder name.

task_completion.description: The new folder 'Work' is successfully created under 'Local Folders'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main folder pane, a folder named 'Work' is clearly visible as a sub-item or child node under the 'Local Folders' account.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the email client to create the folder.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the email client's menus, dialogs, and folder pane, rather than terminal commands (like mkdir) being executed in a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine email client application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used has standard email client features (inbox, compose button, message list), rather than being a text editor or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: The text 'Work' was entered into a folder creation dialog, not a search bar or unrelated field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The input field containing 'Work' is clearly labeled for folder creation (e.g., 'Name:', 'Folder Name:'), not a general search or filter box.
